requirements: Make each job idempotent and safe to re-run on failure.
Use a stable job id or external key to deduplicate retries.
Set bounded retries with exponential backoff and a dead-letter destination.
Persist enough context inside the job payload to survive code changes.
Add timeouts; jobs must not hang the worker.
Add tests for happy path, transient failure (retry), and permanent failure.
security: do not include secrets in job payloads stored in queues
validate inputs popped off the queue as if from the public boundary
performance: batch work where possible; avoid one job per row in tight loops
size the worker pool to the slowest dependency
verify: unit test the job class/function in isolation
smoke-run the worker with a single test job end-to-end
